segments.text: I rise to adjourn today's meeting in memory of Kathy Wooten, a lifelong member of the Watts community and a tireless advocate for victims of violence and their families.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: Kathy was married for 25 years to Patrick Wooten and was a devoted mother to six children, Ricky, Terrence, Patrick, Bridget, Brandon, and Kajuan.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: In 2008, tragedy struck when two of her sons, Brandon and Kejuon, lost their lives to street violence.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: Many of us can only imagine the pain and depth of that loss.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: However, Kathy, as a woman of God, turned her grief into purpose by ensuring that families in similar situations would not have to grieve alone.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: As a tribute to her sons, she founded Loving Hands Community Care.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: Through her organization, Kathy created her own community of healing by helping grieving mothers, siblings, and community members to make them feel heard and safe.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: She was well known in the community for organizing her signature Mother's Day brunch, where mothers who lost children to violence gathered together on Mother's Day to celebrate a day that is often so painful for them.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: I had the pleasure of being with her on several Mother's Day events.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: The most recent one, Kathy was clearly sick, and she had been summoning her strength to be strength to others.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: And she was there for the women who were in attendance, hundreds of women in attendance at that event.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: Loving Hands Community Care also offered several year-round programs in Watts, including a monthly grief support group, a mentorship program, and resources for families in need.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: Apart from her own foundation, Kathy was deeply involved in the Mayor's Grid program, where she served as a victim's advocate.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: She was also in the inaugural cohort of the UCLA Watts Leadership Institute.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: In addition, she was honored in 2021 at SoFi Stadium where her organization was given a $50,000 grant by the LA Super Bowl Host Committee Legacy Champions Program.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: Kathy will always be remembered for her unwavering commitment to others in spite of her own pain.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: She was just a phone call away to so many, ready to help in any way that she could.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: Her passing is a tremendous loss in the community of Watts.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: We wanted to share that her services are pending, but tomorrow on Wednesday, September 10th, there will be a candlelight vigil in her honor from 5 to 7 p.m. at Ted Watkins Park.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: On behalf of the City of Los Angeles and the beloved community of Watts, we extend our deepest condolences to the family, the friends, and all of the people whose lives she touched and forever changed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06

segments.text: May she rest in peace and power.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_06
